info.title: Twilio API definition for public-api voice

type.description: The role of this participant in the conversation.

address.description:
The phone number or identifier for this participant (E.164 format for phone numbers).
Used to correlate this participant with their profile and conversation history.

audioChannelIndex.description: One-based index of the audio channel in a multi-channel recording

transcriptionConfigurationId.description: The ID of the transcription configuration to use

inputSource.description: Discriminator indicating the input source type

sourceId.description:
The SID or TTID of the source audio to transcribe (e.g. a Twilio Recording SID).
When provided, audioStartedAt is inferred from the recording's start time
and does not need to be supplied by the caller.

participants.description: Array of participants in the conversation

transcriptionConfigurationId.description: The ID of the transcription configuration to use

inputSource.description: Discriminator indicating the input source type

mediaUrl.description: URL to the media file to transcribe

audioStartedAt.description: The start time of the audio recording

participants.description:
Participants in the conversation. If omitted or partially specified, defaults
from the transcription configuration will be applied.

url.description: The URL to call when transcription status changes

method.description: The HTTP method to use for the callback, currently only POST is supported

events.description: The transcription events that will trigger the callback

transcriptionEngine.description: The engine used for transcription (Deepgram, Google, or auto)

speechModel.description: The speech model used for transcription (e.g., nova-2, nova-3, chirp_2)

participantDefaults.description: Default participant configurations for the transcription

audioChannelIndex.description: One-based index of the audio channel

id.description: Unique identifier for a Transcription. This is also the transcriptionId returned in the LRO 202 response.

status.description: The current status of the transcription operation

transcriptionConfigurationId.description: Unique identifier for a Transcription configuration.

sourceId.description: The source ID (recording ID) - used for tracking only

audioStartedAt.description:
The call/recording start time.
When the transcription was created using a sourceId, this value is inferred
from the recording resource's start time. When created using a mediaUrl,
this reflects the value supplied by the caller.

conversationId.description: Maestro conversation ID, populated once the transcription has been stored in Maestro.

participants.description: Array of participants in the conversation

updatedAt.description: When this transcript was last updated

voice.v3.transcription.LongRunningOperat.description:
Status response for a transcription operation. Returns the current state of the
operation along with the full transcription resource. Poll this response to track
progress from acceptance through to completion or failure.

operationId.description: Unique identifier for the transcription operation.

status.description:
Current status of the transcription operation.
PENDING: accepted but not yet started.
RUNNING: currently in progress.
COMPLETED: successfully completed.
FAILED: failed and cannot be completed.

statusUrl.description: URL to poll for the latest operation status.

voice.v3.transcription.LongRunningOperat.description:
Response envelope for long-running operations (202 Accepted pattern).
Returned immediately on acceptance and on each status poll. Extensible to allow
additional fields in future versions.

status.description:
Current status of the long-running operation.
PENDING: accepted but not yet started.
RUNNING: currently in progress.
COMPLETED: successfully completed.
FAILED: failed and cannot be completed.

statusUrl.description:
URI to poll for operation status. Mirrors the Location response header.
Provided as a body field for programmatic access by JSON-parsing clients
(RFC 9110 Section 15.3.3).

get.summary: Fetch metadata about a specific transcription

parameters.name: transcriptionId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the transcription to fetch

Retry-After.description: Recommended number of seconds to wait before polling again if status is PENDING or RUNNING

schema.description:
The error response from a Twilio service, compliant with RFC-9457 (Problem Details for HTTP APIs).
See https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9457.html for the specification.

type.description:
A URI reference (RFC3986) that identifies the problem type. This URI should resolve to
human-readable documentation for the problem type. When dereferenced, it SHOULD provide
human-readable documentation for the problem type (e.g., using HTML).
For Twilio errors, this should be a URL to https://www.twilio.com/docs/api/errors/{code}

title.description:
A short, human-readable summary of the problem type. It SHOULD NOT change from occurrence
to occurrence of the problem, except for purposes of localization.

status.description:
The HTTP status code generated by the origin server for this occurrence of the problem.
This is the same as the HTTP status code returned in the response.

detail.description:
A human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence of the problem. This field's value
can change between different occurrences of the same problem type to provide specific details.
detail.example: The 'PhoneNumber' parameter is required but was not provided.

instance.description:
A URI reference that identifies the specific occurrence of the problem. It may or may not
yield further information if dereferenced. This can be used for correlation and debugging.

code.description:
Twilio-specific error code (extension member). This numeric code uniquely identifies
the error type within Twilio's error taxonomy and can be used for programmatic handling.
Registered at https://www.twilio.com/docs/api/errors

errors.description:
Twilio-specific extension for validation errors (typically HTTP 400/422).
Contains an array of individual field-level validation errors, each with a
human-readable detail message and a JSON Pointer (RFC 6901) indicating the
location of the error in the request. This is particularly useful for
request validation failures where multiple fields may have errors.

detail.description: A human-readable explanation of the validation error for this specific field.
detail.example: must be a positive integer

pointer.description: A JSON Pointer (RFC 6901) to the location in the request where the error occurred.

post.summary: Creates a new transcription from either a sourceId or a mediaUrl. Either sourceId or mediaUrl must be provided, but not both.

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: A unique key to ensure idempotency. We recommend using UUID v7. Requests with the same key within the idempotency window return the original response.

202.description:
Transcription request accepted and will be processed asynchronously.
Poll the statusUrl (or Location header) to track progress.

Operation-Id.description:
Server-generated unique identifier for the transcription (TTID format).
This value is also returned as transcription.id in the response body and as the
transcriptionId path parameter for the GET /Transcriptions/{transcriptionId} endpoint.
Use statusUrl (or the Location response header) to poll for operation progress.

Location.description:
URI of the status monitoring endpoint for this operation (RFC 9110 Section 10.2.2).
Poll this URL to check progress.

Retry-After.description: Recommended number of seconds to wait before polling again (RFC 9110 Section 10.2.3)

Idempotency-Key.description: Echoes the Idempotency-Key from the request, if provided.
